bns:Component.name: Main: NYU Langone - Employee Onboarding Orchestration
bns:Component.bns:description: DEMO: Employee Onboarding Orchestration for NYU Langone HealthThis process demonstrates Boomi across all major connector categories - Application, Database, REST API, and Managed File Transfer - in a real NYU Langone scenario. The process has two logical stages:STAGE 1 - COLLECT AND ENRICH (linear):Salesforce returns new hire records. Boomi extracts the employee details, then queries SQL Server to enrich the record with cost center, manager chain, building location, and job family. Once the complete employee profile is assembled, the process branches.STAGE 2 - BRANCH: DELIVER TO FOUR SYSTEMS (4 paths from one enriched record):Branch 1 - ServiceNow: Build IT onboarding ticket payload, POST to ServiceNow REST APIBranch 2 - Workday HCM: Build worker record payload, POST to Workday REST APIBranch 3 - Oracle: Build compliance audit JSON, INSERT to Oracle ONBOARDING_LOG tableBranch 4 - Benefits Vendor: Generate CSV flat file, set FTP filename, deliver via FTP connector--- TALKING POINTS BY STEP ---STAGE 1 - SALESFORCE (Application Connector):"The process starts in Salesforce. Boomi runs a SOQL query to pull new hires where Status equals Hired. You can schedule this hourly or fire it the moment a record changes. For this demo we are using a realistic mock response so you can see the complete flow without live credentials."STAGE 1 - SQL SERVER (Database Connector):"Before we do anything else, Boomi enriches the Salesforce record by querying your SQL Server HR database. The department code from Salesforce becomes a parameterized query input - no string concatenation, no SQL injection risk. We get back cost center, building location, manager hierarchy, and job family in one call."STAGE 2 - THE BRANCH:"Once Boomi has the complete employee profile - data from Salesforce enriched with SQL Server - it fans out into four separate delivery paths. ServiceNow gets the IT ticket. Workday gets the HR record. Oracle gets the compliance log. The benefits vendor gets the enrollment file. All four paths process from the same enriched document. This is where a platform approach shows its value: one source record, four downstream systems, zero duplicate lookups."BRANCH 1 - SERVICENOW (REST Application Connector):"Branch one builds the ServiceNow Service Catalog Request payload and POSTs it to the ServiceNow REST API. One call creates the full IT onboarding ticket - laptop, badge, system access - with every field pre-populated from the employee record."BRANCH 2 - WORKDAY HCM (REST Application Connector):"Branch two calls Workday HCM to provision the official worker record. Same REST connector technology as ServiceNow, just pointing at a different base URL. Your HR team does not have to key anything in manually - the record is there before they know the hire happened."BRANCH 3 - ORACLE (Second Database Connector):"Branch three writes a compliance audit record to Oracle. Notice this is the second database connector in the process - this time Oracle instead of SQL Server. Boomi treats them identically: swap the JDBC driver and connection string, the design pattern is unchanged. The audit record captures which systems were touched, what tickets were created, and an exact timestamp. This is your HIPAA audit trail."BRANCH 4 - BENEFITS VENDOR FILE (FTP Connector):"Branch four generates a CSV flat file in the exact format your benefits vendor expects and delivers it via Boomi's FTP connector. The file is named automatically with the employee ID and date. The benefits vendor picks it up from their FTP endpoint and initiates enrollment. The entire pipeline - Salesforce trigger to FTP file drop - runs without a single human touchpoint."--- CONNECTOR SHOWCASE ---- Salesforce Connector (Application - branded, requires GUI OAuth setup)- Database V2 x2: SQL Server (Stage 1 enrichment) + Oracle (Branch 3 audit)- REST Connector x2: ServiceNow (Branch 1) + Workday HCM (Branch 2)- FTP Connector: Branch 4 FTP delivery to benefits vendorTotal: 5 connector instances, 4 distinct connector types, 0 lines of custom code

shape.name: shape1
shape.userlabel: Start: Scheduled Trigger or Manual Run

shape.name: shape3
shape.userlabel: [DEMO] Salesforce Returns: New Hire - Jennifer Torres, RN II, Cardiac Cath Lab

message.msgTxt: '[{"Id": "003XT000001NYUDEMO","FirstName": "Jennifer","LastName": "Torres","Email": "jennifer.torres@nyulangone.org","Department": "Cardiac Catheterization Lab","Department_Code__c": "CARD-CATH","Hire_Date__c": "2026-03-15","Title": "Registered Nurse II","Manager_Id__c": "MGR2847","Building__c": "Tisch Hospital","Onboarding_Sent__c": false}]'

shape.name: shape4
shape.userlabel: Extract Employee Details from Salesforce (Name, Email, Dept, Hire Date)

shape.name: shape5
shape.userlabel: Build SQL Server Department Lookup Query (Parameterized Input)

shape.name: shape6
shape.userlabel: SQL Server: Enrich Record with Dept, Cost Center, Building, Manager

shape.name: shape7
shape.userlabel: Capture SQL Server Enrichment (Cost Center, Building, Job Family)

shape.name: shape27
shape.userlabel: Enrich Record with Dept, Cost Center, Building, Manager [Map to Canonical Structure & Set [DDP]: BenfitsEligible]

shape.name: shape31
shape.userlabel: Create Incident only if they are not [DDP] BenefitsEligible

decision.name: Create Incident only if they are not [DDP] BenefitsEligible

shape.name: shape32
shape.userlabel: Create Incident only if they are [DDP] BenefitsEligible

decision.name: Create Incident only if they are [DDP] BenefitsEligible
